note: Metadata only. The app does not generate audio, call any TTS/ElevenLabs API, or hold an API key. One row = one MP3.

context.voicePersona: Warm, confident British pub-quiz host — friendly, a little playful, never shouty.
context.responsibleWording: Required for alcohol-sponsor references (e.g. 'please drink responsibly, 18+').

sponsorRules: Use the sponsor/product naturally — don't sound like an advert every time.
Always keep responsible wording for alcohol references (drink responsibly, 18+).
Never invent product facts (ABV, origin, ingredients, awards, brewing history).
Sponsor-question scripts may say 'tonight's featured sponsor/product' generically.
Market-specific sponsor terms must match the active context (UK → Fuller's / London Pride).
sponsorSafeExamples: Tonight's quiz is brought to you with Fuller's and London Pride.
A quick nod to tonight's featured pour before we continue.
Enjoy responsibly, and keep your answers sharper than your arguments.
howToRulesNote: Multiple-choice is the live question format (seeded questions carry options). Typed-answer is product direction — phrase it as 'where enabled'. Don't overpromise features not built.

rows.voiceStyle: British pub host — warm, confident, playful, never shouty
rows.toneNotes: Premium-but-approachable; smiling; not a game-show announcer.
rows.deliveryNotes: Unhurried, clear; leave a breath before answers; consistent loudness for a noisy room.
rows.notes: Build energy to 'let's play!'. Keeps the responsible-sponsor line. No factual product claims.
rows.scriptText: Good evening and welcome to O'Learys for tonight's quiz, brought to you by Fuller's. Scan the QR code on your table, give your team a name, and answer on your phone — one shared answer per team. Please drink responsibly. Phones ready — let's play!

rows.voiceStyle: British pub host — warm, confident, playful, never shouty
rows.toneNotes: Premium-but-approachable; smiling; not a game-show announcer.
rows.deliveryNotes: Unhurried, clear; leave a breath before answers; consistent loudness for a noisy room.
rows.notes: Setup mode: TV + audio — QR shown on the TV / main screen (and table). One device per team. Multiple choice is the live format; typed answer is product direction ('where enabled').
rows.scriptText: Scan the QR code on the main screen, any TV showing the quiz join code, or the QR on your table — or a nearby table. One person from each team joins on their phone, enters your team name, and submits the team's answers. Just follow the prompts on your phone — some questions are multiple choice, others ask for a typed answer where enabled. I'll take you through all the questions first, we'll review the answers later, and announce the winner by team number. No shouting out!

rows.voiceStyle: British pub host — warm, confident, playful, never shouty
rows.toneNotes: Premium-but-approachable; smiling; not a game-show announcer.
rows.deliveryNotes: Unhurried, clear; leave a breath before answers; consistent loudness for a noisy room.
rows.notes: Setup mode: Audio-only — no TV; host reads the questions aloud and gives the join code verbally (digits). One device per team.
rows.scriptText: We're playing without a screen tonight, so listen up. On your phone, head to the join page, and I'll read out tonight's join code — it's one, one, five, six, seven, eight. One person from each team enters the code, picks your team name, and answers on the phone. I'll read every question aloud first, we'll go through the answers later, and the winner's announced by team number.

rows.voiceStyle: British pub host — warm, confident, playful, never shouty

rows.deliveryNotes: Unhurried, clear; leave a breath before answers; consistent loudness for a noisy room.
rows.notes: Setup mode: Local host · mic/speaker · phones-only — lowest-friction; QR on a table card or a host-read join code. One device per team.
rows.scriptText: Nice and simple tonight — phones only. Scan the QR code on your table card, or I'll read out a join code for you to type in. One person from each team joins, enters your team name, and answers on the phone. I'll read the questions over the mic, we'll review the answers afterwards, and announce the winner by team number.
